rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions and helper declarations.
Delete invalid clauses that reference non-existent or mismatched types.
generic_rules: Validate all AGREE references against the AADL model's visible features and types.
Preserve AADL scope rules when generating or modifying AGREE contracts.
Avoid introducing new data types or packages not declared in the model.
Use only helper functions and variables that are explicitly defined in the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes should contain assumptions and guarantees for ports.
Implementation annexes should contain bindings and assignments for subcomponents.
Numeric variables must be assigned numeric expressions; Boolean variables must be assigned Boolean expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not generate AGREE expressions that reference AADL properties as variables.
Do not assume that AADL property associations are automatically available in AGREE scope.
Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers without matching `with` clauses.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type references
Model fusion or architecture insertion locations
